claims.claim_text: In 2021 Zillow wound down Zillow Offers, with over $500 million in write-downs/losses and about 25% workforce reduction.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: Zillow's investor-relations release documents the wind-down, approximate 25% workforce reduction, and the hundreds of millions of related losses, but the primary source is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: MemGPT originated as a UC Berkeley research project in October 2023 and later evolved into Letta.
claims.span_hint: section: Letta (formerly MemGPT): Persistent Memory Paging, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The project site directly ties MemGPT to UC Berkeley and the later Letta lineage, but that primary source is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Using a 1 million-token context for every interaction is slow, harms reasoning, and is prohibitively expensive.
claims.span_hint: section: Letta (formerly MemGPT): Persistent Memory Paging, paragraph 1
claims.anchor_text: Furthermore, filling a massive context window (like 1 million tokens) for every single interaction is incredibly slow, degrades reasoning performance, and is prohibitively expensive.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Furthermore, stuffing very large prompts into every interaction can be slow, costly, and can hurt model performance, so long-running systems usually need retrieval or memory management rather than brute-force context stuffing.
claims.rationale: The exact token count and the absolute performance/cost claim are not backed by a specific primary source here.

claims.claim_text: Letta uses core, archival, and recall-style memory mechanisms, and agents manage memory through explicit memory tools.
claims.span_hint: section: Letta (formerly MemGPT): Persistent Memory Paging, Core/Archival/Recall list plus follow-up paragraph
claims.rationale: Letta's official memory-management docs cover these mechanisms directly, but the docs host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: 8192 tokens is the optimal recall zone for most GPT-4-class models, and longer prompts trigger lost-in-the-middle failures.
claims.span_hint: section: Letta (formerly MemGPT): Persistent Memory Paging, paragraph after code example
claims.anchor_text: Additionally, 8192 tokens represents the optimal recall zone for most GPT-4 class models; beyond this, the "lost in the middle" phenomenon frequently occurs where the model ignores critical instructions buried in the center of the prompt.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Additionally, a deliberately small working context forces the agent to rely on retrieval and summarization instead of endlessly growing the prompt, and very long prompts can suffer from "lost in the middle" failures.
claims.rationale: The general long-context failure mode is citable, but the exact 8192-token optimization claim for most GPT-4-class models is not.

claims.claim_text: ReAct is a named method for interleaving reasoning and acting in language models.
claims.span_hint: section: The Evolution from Pipelines to OS-Like Agents, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The original ReAct paper is the primary source for the named method.

claims.claim_text: AutoGen v0.4 rewrote the framework around an asynchronous, event-driven architecture and topic/subscription messaging model.
claims.span_hint: section: AutoGen 0.4+: Event-Driven Multi-Agent Systems, first 2 paragraphs
claims.rationale: The official AutoGen docs describe the event-driven topic/subscription model directly, but the docs host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: `human_input_mode="NEVER"` disables human prompts in AutoGen, while `TERMINATE` and `ALWAYS` can request user input.
claims.span_hint: section: AutoGen 0.4+: Event-Driven Multi-Agent Systems, paragraph after supervisor code block
claims.rationale: AutoGen's human-input documentation defines these modes explicitly, but the official docs host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: In CrewAI 0.5+, the Hierarchical process automatically provisions a managerial LLM to oversee the execution of tasks.
claims.span_hint: section: CrewAI 0.5+: Role-Based Concurrent Orchestration, paragraph 2
claims.anchor_text: In CrewAI 0.5+, the Hierarchical process automatically provisions a managerial LLM to oversee the execution of tasks.
claims.suggested_rewrite: In CrewAI's Hierarchical process, a manager agent or manager model oversees task execution and delegation.
claims.rationale: CrewAI docs support the hierarchical-manager behavior, but the exact `0.5+` version threshold is not established by an allowlisted primary source in hand.

claims.claim_text: CrewAI's hierarchical process requires `manager_llm` or `manager_agent` to define the manager that coordinates tasks.
claims.span_hint: section: CrewAI 0.5+: Role-Based Concurrent Orchestration, code example and surrounding prose
claims.rationale: CrewAI's processes docs state this requirement directly, but the docs host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: LangGraph models workflows as graphs with explicit nodes/edges and a shared typed state passed between nodes.
claims.span_hint: section: LangGraph and Deterministic Orchestration, both paragraphs
claims.rationale: The LangGraph API reference documents the graph/state model directly, but that reference host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes liveness probes can restart a container when an exec health check fails.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: Deploying Autonomous Agents to Kubernetes, paragraph after YAML block
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes docs explicitly describe exec-based liveness checks and container restarts on probe failure.

claims.claim_text: The MemGPT paper was published on arXiv in October 2023.
claims.rationale: The arXiv record shows the initial MemGPT submission date in October 2023.

claims.claim_text: AutoGen was open-sourced by Microsoft in September 2023 and reached over 20,000 GitHub stars within three months.
claims.anchor_text: 2. AutoGen was open-sourced by Microsoft in September 2023 and rapidly accumulated over 20,000 GitHub stars within its first three months, signaling a massive shift in industry focus toward multi-agent systems.
claims.suggested_rewrite: 2. AutoGen was open-sourced by Microsoft in 2023 and quickly drew substantial developer interest in multi-agent systems.
claims.rationale: The exact three-month star-count claim is not backed by a primary source available in scope.

claims.claim_text: CrewAI can reduce total completion time by up to 35% when switching from sequential to asynchronous delegation with five or more specialists.
claims.anchor_text: 3. CrewAI processes can reduce total task completion time by up to 35 percent when switching from sequential execution to asynchronous delegation among five or more specialized agents.
claims.suggested_rewrite: 3. CrewAI's concurrent delegation model can shorten end-to-end runtime in some workloads compared with purely sequential execution.
claims.rationale: The exact percentage and workload framing are presented as measured results without a primary benchmark source.

claims.claim_text: Early 2023 models typically had 4,096-token contexts, motivating paging systems for documents over 100,000 words.
claims.anchor_text: 4. The maximum context window of early 2023 models was typically 4,096 tokens, which drove the initial development of OS-like memory paging systems to handle documents exceeding 100,000 words.
claims.suggested_rewrite: 4. Early chat models had much smaller context windows than today's long-context models, which helped motivate external-memory and retrieval-based agent architectures.
claims.rationale: The exact token and word-count figures are not sourced to a primary reference here.

further_reading.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary paper behind the OS-style memory analogy used throughout the module.

further_reading.title: Letta GitHub Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Open-source framework that grew out of MemGPT and shows the current stateful-agent project.

further_reading.title: AutoGen GitHub Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Official repository overview for AutoGen's modern layered architecture and agent framework.

further_reading.title: LangGraph GitHub Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Official repository overview for LangGraph's graph-based, long-running agent orchestration model.

further_reading.title: Configure Liveness, Readiness and Startup Probes
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary Kubernetes reference for the health-check behavior discussed in the deployment section.

notes: Quiz prompts, hands-on exercise steps, and code blocks were excluded per instructions. Several framework-specific claims are marked `needs_allowlist_expansion` because their primary docs live on non-allowlisted hosts such as `docs.letta.com`, `docs.crewai.com`, and `microsoft.github.io`.
